inputs.label: Paint Type
inputs.businessContext: Different paint types have different coverage rates and costs per liter. Based on industry standards (ASTM D2805).

inputs.label: Surface Area to Paint
inputs.businessContext: Total area to be painted. Measured in square meters per ISO 9001 quality standards.

inputs.label: Coverage per Liter
inputs.businessContext: Coverage rate depends on paint type and surface porosity. Typical values from manufacturer datasheets (ASTM D2805).

inputs.label: Cost per Liter
inputs.businessContext: Purchase cost of paint per liter. Market prices vary by brand and quality.

inputs.label: Number of Coats
inputs.businessContext: Typically 2 coats for standard coverage. More coats may be needed for dark colors or porous surfaces.

inputs.label: Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage for painters. Includes overhead per industrial engineering standards (Maynard).

inputs.label: Labor Productivity
inputs.businessContext: Area painted per hour per worker. Based on time studies (Salvendy).

inputs.label: Waste Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of paint wasted due to overspray, spillage, etc. Lean manufacturing principles target waste reduction.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data. Affects output adjustment for risk analysis.

rules: if paintType == 'oil' then coveragePerLiter <= 15 (typical oil coverage lower)

thresholds.wasteFactor > 20: High waste factor indicates inefficiency; consider process improvement (Lean).
thresholds.coveragePerLiter < 8: Low coverage may indicate poor paint quality or incorrect application.
thresholds.laborProductivity < 10: Low productivity; consider training or method improvement (Six Sigma).

suggestedActions: wasteFactor > 20 ? 'Implement waste reduction techniques (e.g., proper spray settings, training)' : ''
coveragePerLiter < 8 ? 'Consider higher quality paint or surface preparation' : ''
laborProductivity < 10 ? 'Provide training or use more efficient tools' : ''
